error_map.hint: tally api_key is missing or invalid; re-run pm credentials add tally
tally account lacks access for this request
tally rate limit exceeded (100 requests/minute); the connector will retry with backoff

conformance.reason: cursor_advances' generic re-read harness is not fan_out-aware: it seeds a capture server that always answers 200 with an empty body, but this stream's fan_out.ids_from.request issues its own preliminary GET /forms id-listing request FIRST — against that same always-empty capture server, so zero form ids ever resolve and the per-form submissions sub-sequence (the request that would actually carry startDate) is never issued at all; the harness's single captured-value slot would in any case only ever record whichever request shape hit it last. This is a harness limitation (checkCursorAdvances has no fan_out-aware two-phase variant), not a bundle defect; hook-covered-style proof is not applicable (no hook here) but the identical marker mechanism applies to any dynamic check a generic harness genuinely cannot reach. Proven live by internal/connectors/paritytest/tally/parity_test.go's TestParityTally_SubmissionsFanOutSendsStartDateOnResumedSync (asserts the real two-phase read sends startDate=<seeded cursor> on the per-form sub-sequence) and TestParityTally_SubmissionsFreshSyncOmitsStartDate (asserts a fresh sync omits the param entirely).
